dominant_palette: deep crimson red, matte black, off-white/cream, dark oxblood, silver-grey
secondary_palette: warm gold, translucent gemstone colours (blue, rose, amber)
composition_vocabulary: Upper tiles favour centred iconic placement — a single sculptural object (horse, ring) isolated against black or flooded red with typography as texture. Lower tiles shift into archival grid logic: product-card layouts with handwritten annotations, small photographs mounted on white paper. The batch oscillates between poster-grade graphic authority and quiet documentary intimacy.
lighting_quality: Predominantly high-contrast studio and graphic light — either flooded flat colour or controlled spot-lit object photography; no natural or ambient light present.
material_textures: lacquered dark resin, polished sterling silver, matte printed paper, translucent coloured gemstone, brushed gold, archival card stock
aesthetic_register: The sample sits at the intersection of contemporary art-school graphic design and artisan jewellery documentation — campaign identity language (bold colour floods, typographic texture) layered over an almost archival, cataloguing sensibility for the jewellery objects.
notably_absent: Sparkle-forward gemstone photography, lifestyle or on-body model shots, and the soft aspirational styling (velvet risers, bokeh backgrounds, manicured hands) that dominate conventional luxury jewellery moodboards.
